name: Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando
mission: The mission of the Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando is to provide quality education of the highest standard in a safe, supportive, dynamic, and engaging Islamic environment so that students have the opportunity to excel to their fullest potential.
theoryOfChange: By providing a dynamic student-centered educational environment with high academic standards and expectations, Islamic values and morals, students will develop a sense of purpose, a connection to ALLAH, and a desire to serve their creator via service to others and their community.
scoreSummary: Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando Inc shows moderate impact and moderate alignment (significant risk deductions applied).

case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="16">lack of a multi-member independent board</cite>, which is a standard requirement for non-profit governance. Additionally, the <cite id="18">absence of zakat-eligibility</cite> may not meet the needs of donors looking to fulfill specific religious duties.

confidence.notes: Financial data is fresh, but governance and impact metrics are limited.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust the academic quality due to <cite id="7">Cognia accreditation</cite> and <cite id="5">high Niche ratings</cite>. However, the <cite id="16">single-member board</cite> undermines traditional non-profit accountability standards.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization should expand its board to at least five independent members to ensure robust oversight.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains <cite id="8">15.6 months of working capital</cite>, significantly exceeding industry averages and ensuring long-term operational sustainability.
MAGO operates with a <cite id="11">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 spent</cite> to raise every $1 in contributions, maximizing the impact of donor gifts.

strengths_deep_dive: MAGO's primary strength lies in its ability to scale its faith-based educational model while maintaining fiscal discipline. The institution has achieved a <cite id="4">25.5% revenue CAGR</cite> over the last three years, growing its net assets to <cite id="12">$3,270,864 by 2024</cite>. This growth is supported by a <cite id="13">documented theory of change</cite> that integrates academic rigor with Islamic values. Furthermore, the school's <cite id="14">67.8% program expense ratio</cite> reported in recent filings indicates a total commitment of resources toward its educational mission, including <cite id="15">professional development and service-learning projects</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando (MAGO) is a faith-based educational institution that has served the Florida community since <cite id="1">its founding in 2006</cite>. The academy provides a comprehensive academic track ranging from <cite id="2">Pre-K through Grade 8</cite>, focusing on a mission of virtue, wisdom, and strength. In the 2024 fiscal year, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $3,572,587</cite>, representing a significant <cite id="4">three-year revenue growth rate of 25.5%</cite>. MAGO distinguishes itself through academic excellence, having been recognized as an <cite id="5">A+ rated school by Niche</cite> and ranking within the <cite id="6">top 20% of private schools in Florida</cite>. The institution is <cite id="7">accredited by Cognia</cite>, ensuring that its educational standards meet rigorous external benchmarks. Financially, the academy maintains <cite id="8">15.6 months of working capital</cite>, providing a robust buffer for operational stability. While the school generates <cite id="9">$479,477 in program service revenue</cite> through tuition, it relies heavily on <cite id="10">$3,093,110 in contributions</cite> to sustain its mission and service-learning projects.
